extraction.one_line_summary: FUGELSANG Jakob attacked over the final climb (1.3km Côte de la Roche-aux-Faucons, 13km to go), shed Formolo and Woods on descent, survived massive slide.
tactical_patterns: Van Avermaet attacked repeatedly on Côte de la Locked (74km out) to fatigue favorites before final climb selection.
Movistar accelerated hard after Valverde abandoned (103km to go), revealing team shifts strategy when captain drops.
Fugelsang's winning move came 200m after Côte de la Roche-aux-Faucons summit, not on climb itself—descent attack caught favorites recovering.
Wellens initiated final selection at climb base for Lotto-Soudal, demonstrating domestique-led selection attempts when team lacks pure climber.

rider_signals.FUGELSANG Jakob: positive: consistent spring podiums across all classics preceding this win suggests peaking strategy works for stage racers in monuments
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: negative: visibly cracked on Roche-aux-Faucons, worked backward through group—unusual vulnerability on short steep finale he typically dominates
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: multiple testing attacks at 74km suggests one-day specialists probe earlier on cold/wet days, but lacked finishing power
rider_signals.WOODS Michael: negative: bridged to lead group over final climb but immediately shed by Fugelsang's post-summit acceleration—acceleration deficit after sustained efforts
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: negative: abandoned at 103km, Movistar immediately changed tactics—suggests team tactics predetermined around leader presence
rider_signals.FORMOLO Davide: positive: held second behind Fugelsang after summit attack, confirming Barcelona solo stage win showed genuine climbing emergence
